title: The old man who feeds the birds
summary: Describe an old man who comes to the park each morning to feed the birds.
topic: The old man who feeds the birds
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about an old man who feeds birds in your local park. Describe what he looks like, when he comes, and how the birds know him. Use present simple.
hintStarters: There is an old man in the park who…
sampleText: There is an old man in the park near my flat in Krakow. People call him Pan Janek. He is small and thin, and he walks with a wooden cane. Every morning at eight o'clock he comes to the same park bench. He always brings a paper bag of bread crumbs. The birds know him. When he sits down, the pigeons and the sparrows fly to him. Some sparrows even land on his hand. He does not say much. He has a quiet smile. He stays for about one hour, and he gives all the bread to the birds. After that, he stands up slowly and walks home. The children in the park watch him. I think the birds wait for him every day. Sometimes when it rains, he still comes with his umbrella. I see him from my window. He is a small man, but he gives a small gift to the city every morning.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: túi giấy đựng vụn bánh mì
món quà nhỏ cho thành phố

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Using 'when' for time
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'when' nối hai hành động xảy ra cùng lúc hoặc liên tiếp: 'When he sits down, the pigeons and the sparrows fly to him'. Mệnh đề với 'when' có thể đặt ở đầu câu.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'when' links two actions that happen at the same time or one after the other: 'When he sits down, the pigeons and the sparrows fly to him'. The 'when' clause can come at the start.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Adverbs of frequency in the middle
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'always', 'sometimes', 'never' đặt giữa chủ ngữ và động từ chính: 'He always brings a paper bag', 'Sometimes when it rains, he still comes'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'always', 'sometimes', 'never' go between the subject and the main verb: 'He always brings a paper bag', 'Sometimes when it rains, he still comes'.

sampleTranslationVi: Có một ông cụ trong công viên gần căn hộ của tôi ở Krakow. Mọi người gọi ông là Pan Janek. Ông nhỏ bé và gầy, và ông đi với một cây gậy gỗ. Mỗi sáng lúc tám giờ ông đến cùng một chiếc ghế công viên. Ông luôn mang theo một túi giấy đựng vụn bánh mì. Lũ chim biết ông. Khi ông ngồi xuống, chim bồ câu và chim sẻ bay đến với ông. Một số con chim sẻ thậm chí đậu lên tay ông. Ông không nói nhiều. Ông có một nụ cười lặng lẽ. Ông ở lại khoảng một tiếng, và ông cho hết bánh mì cho lũ chim. Sau đó, ông đứng dậy từ từ và đi về nhà. Lũ trẻ trong công viên nhìn ông. Tôi nghĩ lũ chim chờ ông mỗi ngày. Đôi khi trời mưa, ông vẫn đến với cái ô của mình. Tôi nhìn ông từ cửa sổ nhà mình. Ông là một người đàn ông nhỏ bé, nhưng ông tặng một món quà nhỏ cho thành phố mỗi sáng.

mcQuestions.prompt: What time does the old man come to the park?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'Every morning at eight o'clock he comes to the same park bench'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the time in the morning.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does he carry to walk with?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'he walks with a wooden cane'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the thing that helps him walk.

mcQuestions.prompt: What is in the paper bag?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'He always brings a paper bag of bread crumbs'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for what he brings for the birds.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which two kinds of birds come to him?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'the pigeons and the sparrows fly to him'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the two bird names in the same sentence.

mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'Some sparrows even land on his hand'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the part of his body the birds touch.

mcQuestions.prompt: How long does he stay in the park?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'He stays for about one hour'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the length of his visit.

options: When he sits down, the birds fly to him.
When sits he down, the birds fly to him.
He sits down when, the birds fly to him.
When he sits down the birds flies to him.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'When + subject + verb', and the plural 'birds' takes 'fly' not 'flies'.
mcQuestions.hint: Check both the structure after 'when' and the verb form.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does he bring when it rains?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'Sometimes when it rains, he still comes with his umbrella'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple sentence.
options: He give all the bread to the birds.
He gives all the bread to the birds.
He giving all the bread to the birds.
He is give all the bread to the birds.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the writer think the birds do every day?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I think the birds wait for him every day'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the writer's opinion about the birds.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do small daily acts, like feeding birds in a park, matter for a city? What does this tell us about being a good neighbor?
